name: Chicken and Potato Wedges W/Lemn-Dill Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1 pound red potatoes, cut into 1/2-inch thick wedges
4 boneless skinless chicken breasts, cut in half
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large saucepan, combine 6 cups of water, potato wedges, and 1/2 teaspoon salt.
2. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat to medium; cook for 10 minutes or until potatoes are tender. Drain well.
3. To flatten each chicken breast half, place the boned side up between two pieces of plastic wrap or waxed paper. Working from the center, gently pound the chicken with a flat side of a mallet or rolling pin until about 1/4-inch thick. Remove the wrap.
4. Spray a 2-inch nonstick skillet with nonstick cooking spray and place over medium-high heat. Melt 1 teaspoon of the butter or margarine until bubbly. Add chicken; cook for 6 minutes.
5. Turn the chicken and sprinkle with dill. Cook an additional 3-5 minutes or until chicken is fork-tender and juices run clear. Remove chicken from the skillet and set aside.
6. In the same skillet, combine the remaining 1 tablespoon butter or margarine, water, lemon juice, 1/4 teaspoon salt, and pepper. Cook over medium-high heat for 2 minutes, stirring frequently.
7. To serve, place the cooked potatoes on a serving platter. Arrange chicken around the outer edges of the platter. Spoon sauce over top. Sprinkle with parsley.
Makes 4 servings.
Per 1/4 of recipe: 310 calories, 9 g fat, 14% cholesterol, 540 mg sodium, 29 g carbs, 3 g fiber, 29 g protein
Dietary Exchanges: 2 starch, 3 very lean meat, 1 fat
